claim: AI Tagger Universe v1.0.17 (March 2026, 84 GitHub stars) supports batch tagging entire folders or whole vault, generates nested hierarchical tags into frontmatter, and integrates with 15+ providers including Ollama, LM Studio, OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Bedrock, Vertex AI.
quote: Batch tag entire folders or your whole vault... Local LLMs: Ollama, LM Studio, LocalAI... Cloud Services: OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Groq, Grok, Mistral, DeepSeek, Cohere, GLM (Zhipu AI), MiMo, Minimax, SiliconFlow, Aliyun, Bedrock, Vertex AI, OpenRouter

claim: Smart Connections (4.9k GitHub stars, v4.3.0 April 2026) focuses on semantic discovery via local embeddings rather than tagging or frontmatter generation, and shifted to a source-available 'Smart Plugins License' restricting competing offerings.
quote: Source available under Smart Plugins License with restrictions on general-purpose competing Obsidian offerings... The documentation makes no mention of automatic frontmatter generation or auto-tagging capabilities.

claim: Smart Connections users have raised concerns in 2026 about pricing being '10-20x typical paid Obsidian plugin pricing' and a switch to a proprietary license without notification.
quote: Users not informed about switch to proprietary license; other concerns

claim: Copilot for Obsidian (6.8k GitHub stars, v3.2.8 April 29 2026) emphasizes agentic chat, vault-wide RAG, and Composer V2 file editing rather than dedicated auto-tagging or frontmatter backfill; the Plus tier is paid via Brevilabs.
quote: The plugin emphasizes AI-assisted knowledge work rather than automated tagging systems... The search results don't indicate dedicated auto-tagging, frontmatter generation, or batch indexing features as primary functionalities.

claim: Copilot's first 2026 release added web-tab reading, YouTube/web clipper slash commands, Composer V2 with side-by-side diffs, auto-compact for 128k+ context, and Obsidian Bases support.
quote: Composer V2 editing with smarter file editing, Azure provider unification, drag-to-insert wikilinks, Obsidian Bases support, and LM Studio Responses API support

claim: Text Generator (1.9k GitHub stars, v0.8.7 April 27 2026) is a template-driven plugin where reusable prompts plus frontmatter-configurable AI settings let users batch-generate metadata, summaries, or aliases across notes; supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, HuggingFace, local models, and gpt-5/thinking models.
quote: Template Engine: Users can create reusable templates to streamline repetitive tasks, enabling systematic content generation across multiple notes... Frontmatter Configuration: The tool leverages frontmatter for customizable settings

claim: Note Companion (formerly File Organizer 2000; 832 stars, v3.6.18 April 27 2026) provides folder/tag/title/template suggestions plus an inbox-automation mode that processes raw captures in batch, and offers cloud subscription, self-host, or BYO-key access; desktop-only.
quote: Folder, tag, title, and template suggestions; Optional inbox automation for raw notes... Available as Obsidian plugin (desktop-only) with cloud subscription option, self-hosting support, or bring-your-own-API-keys approach

claim: Metadata Auto Classifier (52 stars, v1.12.9 April 2026) is a 2025 entrant that uses OpenAI GPT models to auto-generate both tags and arbitrary frontmatter fields based on customizable classification rules.
quote: AI-powered Obsidian plugin that automatically classifies and generates metadata (tags, frontmatter) for your notes... integrates with OpenAI's GPT models to suggest meaningful metadata

claim: A 2026 hands-on test paired AI Tagger Universe with Auto Note Mover and a locally-hosted Gemma 3 12B via LM Studio; the 'Generate tags for vault' command successfully retro-tagged hundreds of existing notes in under an hour of setup.
quote: running the command Generate tags for vault begins tagging all my notes automatically... organize hundreds of messy notes... entire setup completely changed how I approach note-taking

claim: The 2026 r/ObsidianMD consensus splits AI picks across three lanes rather than crowning one: Smart Composer for fastest writing assist (uses free Llama 3.2), Smart Connections for semantic search/embeddings, and Copilot for vault-wide RAG chat.
quote: Smart Composer (fastest, uses free Llama 3.2) is recommended for writing assistance, Smart Connections for semantic search and embeddings, and Copilot for vault-wide RAG chat

claim: The 2026 Obsidian-AI plugin landscape is splitting into 'retrieval engines, workflow operators, and autonomous agent surfaces', with Note Companion called out as strongest 'when the job is turning chaos into organized notes' and new entrants like Obsilo Agent (55+ tools) and Sonar (local-first) emerging.
quote: Note Companion — Positioned as strongest when the job is turning chaos into organized notes... Obsilo Agent — Described as the most ambitious current entrant, offering 55+ tools, memory layers, and agent-native capabilities

claim: LLM Tagger is a focused Ollama-only plugin that auto-applies tags to newly created or modified notes and added custom Ollama-server configuration, giving fully-local users a lighter alternative to AI Tagger Universe.
quote: LLM Tagger plugin enhances note organization in Obsidian by using locally running large language models via Ollama to automatically generate relevant tags... auto-tagging feature that applies tags to newly created or modified notes

claim: Obsidian-Frontmatter-Generator (46 stars, last updated Nov 2023) is a non-AI template-based fallback that evaluates JS/JSON expressions and includes whole-vault and per-folder commands, useful as a deterministic complement to AI tagging.
quote: Template-based... Users define a frontmatter template (JSON or JavaScript expression)... command to run in folder and in the whole vault, enabling bulk generation across multiple notes
